---
format: html
title: plot_trait_group_distribution()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>plot_trait_group_distribution</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Plot Raw Trait Value Distribution for One Taxon-Domain Group</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Builds a 'ggplot2' strip + boxplot showing every raw trait value for
a single 'taxon_name x trait_domain_name' group, with colour-coded
Tukey fence flags and horizontal fence lines for both the standard
(1.5x IQR) and extreme (3x IQR) thresholds.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>plot_trait_group_distribution(
  data_group_raw,
  data_group_summary,
  sel_taxon,
  sel_domain,
  graphical_options,
  verbose = TRUE
)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="data_group_raw">data_group_raw</code></td>
<td>
<p>A tibble of raw trait observations for one taxon x domain group.
Must contain columns 'trait_value' (numeric), 'trait_name'
(character), and 'trait_domain_name' (character).</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="data_group_summary">data_group_summary</code></td>
<td>
<p>A single-row tibble of per-group QC statistics, as produced by
'generate_trait_qc_report()'. Must contain numeric columns 'mean',
'median', 'IQR', and integer columns 'n_suspected_outliers_taxon'
(integer) and 'outlier_fraction' (numeric).</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="sel_taxon">sel_taxon</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar. Name of the taxon being inspected. Used in the
plot title.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="sel_domain">sel_domain</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar. Name of the trait domain being inspected. Used in
the plot title.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="graphical_options">graphical_options</code></td>
<td>
<p>Named list with elements 'width', 'height', 'units', 'dpi', and
'bg', as returned by 'get_active_config(&quot;graphical&quot;)'. Passed to
'ggview::canvas()'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="verbose">verbose</code></td>
<td>
<p>Logical. If 'TRUE' (default), the computed Tukey fence boundaries
are printed to the console via 'cli::cli_inform()'.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Details</h3>

<p>The function computes Q1, Q3, and IQR from 'data_group_raw' and
derives four fence values:
- inner lower / upper: Q1 - 1.5 * IQR and Q3 + 1.5 * IQR
- outer lower / upper: Q1 - 3 * IQR and Q3 + 3 * IQR
</p>
<p>Each observation is classified as '&quot;within fence&quot;',
'&quot;mild outlier (1.5x IQR)&quot;', or '&quot;extreme outlier (3x IQR)&quot;'.
When 'data_group_raw' contains more than one distinct 'trait_name',
observations are faceted by 'trait_name'; otherwise the x-axis shows
'trait_domain_name'.
</p>
<p>Plot layer order follows project conventions: 'ggplot()' -&gt; facets
-&gt; scales -&gt; labels -&gt; theme -&gt; 'ggview::canvas()' -&gt; geoms.
</p>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>A 'ggplot2' object. The plot is not printed; call 'print()' on the
return value to display it.
</p>


<h3>See Also</h3>

<p>[generate_trait_qc_report()], [apply_trait_corrections()],
[validate_trait_corrections()]
</p>


<h3>Examples</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>## Not run: 
data_raw &lt;-
  data_traits_raw |&gt;
  dplyr::filter(
    taxon_name == "Anacyclus clavatus",
    trait_domain_name == "Leaf Area"
  ) |&gt;
  dplyr::arrange(trait_value)

data_summary &lt;-
  data_qc_report |&gt;
  dplyr::filter(
    taxon_name == "Anacyclus clavatus",
    trait_domain_name == "Leaf Area"
  )

graphical_options &lt;-
  get_active_config("graphical")

p &lt;-
  plot_trait_group_distribution(
    data_group_raw = data_raw,
    data_group_summary = data_summary,
    sel_taxon = "Anacyclus clavatus",
    sel_domain = "Leaf Area",
    graphical_options = graphical_options
  )

base::print(p)

## End(Not run)
</code></pre>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
